verdict_reasoning: TMED9 is a type I single-pass transmembrane protein of the p24/emp24/gp25L family residing in the early secretory pathway, cycling between ER and Golgi as a cargo receptor (COPI/COPII-mediated transport). Its TM domain anchors it in endomembrane compartments, not the PM. Ruling out contextual buckets: no documented cancer/cell-death/activation-induced outer-leaflet ecto-pool for TMED9 specifically; no narrow germline/developmental surface-restricted display; not a lysosomal TM protein undergoing lysosomal exocytosis; no documented PM minority pool or surface-cycling fraction (steady-state is ER-Golgi-ERGIC); no wash-resistant tethering to a TM partner at the PM. Cargo-receptor role is intra-secretory, not surface-delivering.
predicted_key_uncertainty: Cannot fully exclude a minor PM pool during constitutive cargo cycling, but no gene-specific surface-proteomics evidence known.
